name: EDTF
comment:
A small parser for a subset of the Extended Date/Time Format (EDTF) format.
It does not cover the entire specification but aims to cover common cases.

Supported (Level 0 + useful Level 1 bits):

  - Calendar dates: `YYYY`, `YYYY-MM`, `YYYY-MM-DD`
  - Expanded years via `Y` prefix: `Y-12034`, `Y19999`
  - Seasons (Level 1): `YYYY-21` (Spring), `YYYY-22` (Summer),
    `YYYY-23` (Autumn), `YYYY-24` (Winter)
  - Uncertainty `?` and approximation `~` suffix markers, alone or combined
    in any order (e.g. `2020?`, `2020~`, `2020?~`, `2020-05~?`)
  - Intervals: `<edtf>/<edtf>` where either side can be a real date or
    open using `..` or the word `open` (non-standard but practical)
  - Lists: comma-separated items, e.g. `2020,2021-05,2021-21`

**Not** implemented (for now):

  - Sets/choices with braces `{}` / `..` in sets and the full Level 2
    qualifiers
  - Week dates / ordinal dates / times / time zones

@docs EDTF, DateAnnotated, DateValue, Season, Month

@docs parse, attempt, toString

unions.name: EDTF
unions.comment:
EDTF date types.

An Interval is a specified time period; for example, 1689/1750.
An UnspecifiedInterval is a time period where the start and end are inferred;
for example, 192X = 1920s, 17XX = 1700s, 2020-03-XX is March 2020.

unions.name: Month
unions.comment: Custom type for months of the year

unions.name: Season
unions.comment: Seasons as per EDTF numeric codes 21..24.

aliases.name: DateAnnotated
aliases.comment: A date value with uncertainty/approximation flags.
aliases.type: { date : EDTF.DateValue, uncertain : Basics.Bool, approximate : Basics.Bool }

values.name: attempt
values.comment:
Attempt to parse a string as EDTF.

Unlike `parse`, this never returns parser errors. If parsing succeeds, the
normalized EDTF string is returned (via `toString`). If parsing fails, the
original input string is returned unchanged.

    attempt "2020-05-17"
    -- "2020-05-17"

    attempt "not a date"
    -- "not a date"

values.name: parse
values.comment:
Parse a string into an `EDTF` AST.

    parse "2020-05-17"
        == Ok (Single { date = YMD 2020 5 17, uncertain = False, approximate = False })

    parse "2020?~"
        == Ok (Single { date = Y 2020, uncertain = True, approximate = True })

    parse "2020/.."
        == Ok (Interval (Just { date = Y 2020, uncertain = False, approximate = False }) Nothing)

    parse "2020,2021-21"
        == Ok
            (List
                [ Single { date = Y 2020, uncertain = False, approximate = False }
                , Single { date = Season 2021 Spring, uncertain = False, approximate = False }
                ]
            )

values.name: toString
values.comment: Render an `EDTF` AST back to a normalized string.

name: EDTF.Humanize

values.name: defaultLanguage
values.comment:
Default language used when no explicit preference is provided.

Defaults to `English`.

values.name: fromCode
values.comment:
Parse a language code/name into a `Language`.

Supported values include: `"en"`, `"fr"`, `"de"`, `"it"` and
`"french"`, `"german"`, `"italian"`.
Unknown values fall back to `English`.

values.name: humanize
values.comment:
Render an `EDTF` value as human-readable text in the selected language.

    humanize English (Single { date = Y 1975, uncertain = False, approximate = False })
    -- "1975"

Typically used after parsing:

    parse "1970/1980"
        |> Result.map (humanize English)

values.name: humanizeWith
values.comment:
Render an `EDTF` value as human-readable text with a custom locale.

You can start from a built-in locale and override the fields you need.

    custom =
        { englishLocale | seasonName = \_ -> "temporada" }

    parse "1975-22"
        |> Result.map (humanizeWith custom)

values.name: toCode
values.comment:
Convert a `Language` to its short code.

    toCode German == "de"
